message.content:
Explore two areas of the nginx-gateway-fabric repo (working dir is repo root). Do a "medium-thorough" sweep.

AREA 1 — the CLI entrypoints in `cmd/gateway/`:
- commands.go — what subcommands exist (controller, generate-certs, initialize, sleep, endpoint-picker etc.) and what flags each takes.
- initialize.go — what the `initialize` command does.
- validation.go and validating_types.go — what the `gateway` validation / admission webhook does.
- endpoint_picker.go — what the endpoint-picker command is (likely Gateway API inference extension endpoint picker).
- certs.go — what generate-certs does.

Give the purpose of each command and the key flags/options.

AREA 2 — the API types under `apis/`:
- apis/v1alpha1 — what CRDs/types are defined (list the type names).
- apis/v1alpha2 — what CRDs/types are defined.
- apis/waf/v1 — what the WAFPolicy type looks like.

For each api group, give the Go package path, the resource kinds defined, and a 1-line purpose for the most important ones (e.g. NginxProxy, ClientSettingsPolicy, ObservabilityPolicy, SnippetsFilter, etc.).

Return a concise structured text report with file paths and key type/symbol names. Do NOT dump whole files.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

666	func validatePLMSecretNamespacesWatched(
667		params plmStorageParams,
668		watchNamespaces []string,
669		controllerNamespace string,
670	) error {
671		if params.URL.value == "" {
672			return nil
673		}
674	
675		if len(watchNamespaces) == 0 {
676			return nil
677		}
678	
679		watchedNamespacesSet := make(map[string]struct{}, len(watchNamespaces)+1)
680		for _, namespace := range watchNamespaces {
681			watchedNamespacesSet[namespace] = struct{}{}
682		}
683		// The controller's own namespace is always watched at runtime (see buildManagerCache in
684		// internal/controller/manager.go), so secrets explicitly scoped to it are valid even when
685		// it is not listed in --watch-namespaces.
686		if controllerNamespace != "" {
687			watchedNamespacesSet[controllerNamespace] = struct{}{}
688		}
689	
690		secretFlags := []struct {
691			flagName string
692			value    string
693		}{
694			{flagName: plmStorageCredentialsSecretFlag, value: params.CredentialsSecret.value},
695			{flagName: plmStorageCASecretFlag, value: params.CASecret.value},
696			{flagName: plmStorageClientSSLSecretFlag, value: params.ClientSSLSecret.value},
697		}
698	
699		for _, secretFlag := range secretFlags {
700			namespace, _, hasExplicitNamespace := strings.Cut(secretFlag.value, "/")
701			if !hasExplicitNamespace {
702				continue
703			}
704	
705			if _, ok := watchedNamespacesSet[namespace]; ok {
706				continue
707			}
708	
709			return fmt.Errorf(
710				"--%s references namespace %q, which is not included in --watch-namespaces",
711				secretFlag.flagName,
712				namespace,
713			)
714		}
715	
716		return nil
717	}

990		var flagKeys, flagValues []string
991	
992		flags.VisitAll(
993			func(flag *pflag.Flag) {
994				flagKeys = append(flagKeys, flag.Name)
995	
996				if flag.Value.Type() == "bool" {
997					flagValues = append(flagValues, flag.Value.String())
998				} else {
999					val := "user-defined"
1000					if flag.Value.String() == flag.DefValue {
1001						val = "default"
1002					}
1003	
1004					flagValues = append(flagValues, val)
1005				}
1006			},
1007		)
1008	
1009		return flagKeys, flagValues
1010	}

120	// LoadBalancingType defines the supported load balancing methods.
121	//
122	// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=round_robin;least_conn;ip_hash;hash;hash consistent;random;random two;random two least_conn;random two least_time=header;random two least_time=last_byte;least_time header;least_time last_byte;least_time header inflight;least_time last_byte inflight
123	//
124	//nolint:lll
125	type LoadBalancingType string
126	
127	const (
128		// Combination of NGINX directive
129		// - https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_upstream_module.html#random
130		// - https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_upstream_module.html#least_conn
131		// - https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_upstream_module.html#least_time
132		// - https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_upstream_module.html#upstream
133		// - https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_upstream_module.html#ip_hash
134		// - https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_upstream_module.html#hash
135	
136		// LoadBalancingMethods supported by NGINX OSS and NGINX Plus.
137	
138		// LoadBalancingTypeRoundRobin enables round-robin load balancing,
139		// distributing requests evenly across all upstream servers.
140		LoadBalancingTypeRoundRobin LoadBalancingType = "round_robin"
141	
142		// LoadBalancingTypeLeastConnection enables least-connections load balancing,
143		// routing requests to the upstream server with the fewest active connections.
144		LoadBalancingTypeLeastConnection LoadBalancingType = "least_conn"
145	
146		// LoadBalancingTypeIPHash enables IP hash-based load balancing,
147		// ensuring requests from the same client IP are routed to the same upstream server.
148		LoadBalancingTypeIPHash LoadBalancingType = "ip_hash"
149	
150		// LoadBalancingTypeHash enables generic hash-based load balancing,
151		// routing requests to upstream servers based on a hash of a specified key
152		// HashMethodKey field must be set when this method is selected.
153		// Example configuration: hash $binary_remote_addr;.
154		LoadBalancingTypeHash LoadBalancingType = "hash"
155	
156		// LoadBalancingTypeHashConsistent enables consistent hash-based load balancing,
157		// which minimizes the number of keys remapped when a server is added or removed.
158		// HashMethodKey field must be set when this method is selected.
159		// Example configuration: hash $binary_remote_addr consistent;.
160		LoadBalancingTypeHashConsistent LoadBalancingType = "hash consistent"
161	
162		// LoadBalancingTypeRandom enables random load balancing,
163		// routing requests to upstream servers in a random manner.
164		LoadBalancingTypeRandom LoadBalancingType = "random"
165	
166		// LoadBalancingTypeRandomTwo enables a variation of random load balancing
167		// that randomly selects two servers and forwards traffic to one of them.
168		// The default method is least_conn which passes a request to a server with the least number of active connections.
169		LoadBalancingTypeRandomTwo LoadBalancingType = "random two"
170	
171		// LoadBalancingTypeRandomTwoLeastConnection enables a variation of least-connections
172		// balancing that randomly selects two servers and forwards traffic to the one with
173		// fewer active connections.
174		LoadBalancingTypeRandomTwoLeastConnection LoadBalancingType = "random two least_conn"
175	
176		// LoadBalancingMethods supported by NGINX Plus.
177	
178		// LoadBalancingTypeRandomTwoLeastTimeHeader enables a variation of least-time load balancing
179		// that randomly selects two servers and forwards traffic to the one with the least
180		// time to receive the response header.
181		LoadBalancingTypeRandomTwoLeastTimeHeader LoadBalancingType = "random two least_time=header"
182	
183		// LoadBalancingTypeRandomTwoLeastTimeLastByte enables a variation of least-time load balancing
184		// that randomly selects two servers and forwards traffic to the one with the least time
185		// to receive the full response.
186		LoadBalancingTypeRandomTwoLeastTimeLastByte LoadBalancingType = "random two least_time=last_byte"
187	
188		// LoadBalancingTypeLeastTimeHeader enables least-time load balancing,
189		// routing requests to the upstream server with the least time to receive the response header.
190		LoadBalancingTypeLeastTimeHeader LoadBalancingType = "least_time header"
191	
192		// LoadBalancingTypeLeastTimeLastByte enables least-time load balancing,
193		// routing requests to the upstream server with the least time to receive the full response.
194		LoadBalancingTypeLeastTimeLastByte LoadBalancingType = "least_time last_byte"
195	
196		// LoadBalancingTypeLeastTimeHeaderInflight enables least-time load balancing,
197		// routing requests to the upstream server with the least time to receive the response header,
198		// considering the incomplete requests.
199		LoadBalancingTypeLeastTimeHeaderInflight LoadBalancingType = "least_time header inflight"
200	
201		// LoadBalancingTypeLeastTimeLastByteInflight enables least-time load balancing,
202		// routing requests to the upstream server with the least time to receive the full response,
203		// considering the incomplete requests.
204		LoadBalancingTypeLeastTimeLastByteInflight LoadBalancingType = "least_time last_byte inflight"
205	)
206	
207	// HashMethodKey defines the key used for hash-based load balancing methods.
208	// The key must be a valid NGINX variable name starting with '$' followed by lowercase
209	// letters and underscores only.
210	// For a full list of NGINX variables,
211	// refer to: https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_upstream_module.html#variables
212	//
213	// +kubebuilder:validation:Pattern=`^\$[a-z_]+$`
214	type HashMethodKey string

**Purpose:** Run the NGINX Gateway Fabric control plane

**Key Flags:**
- `--gateway-ctlr-name` (required) - Controller name in format `gateway.nginx.org/PATH`
- `--gatewayclass` (required) - Name of GatewayClass resource

- `--nginx-plus` - Enable NGINX Plus

- `--nginx-one-dataplane-key-secret` - NGINX One Console dataplane key secret
- `--nginx-one-telemetry-endpoint-host/port` - NGINX One Console telemetry endpoint
- `--nginx-one-tls-skip-verify` - Disable TLS verification for NGINX One

- `--health-disable/port` - Health probe server configuration (default: port 8081)
- `--leader-election-disable/lock-name` - Leader election settings
- `--product-telemetry-disable` - Disable product telemetry collection
- `--gateway-api-experimental-features` - Enable Gateway API experimental features
- `--gateway-api-inference-extension` - Enable Gateway API Inference Extension support
- `--snippets` / `--snippets-filters` (deprecated) - Enable NGINX configuration snippets
- `--nginx-scc` - SecurityContextConstraints for OpenShift
- `--watch-namespaces` - Comma-separated list of namespaces to watch
- `--server-tls-domain` - Domain suffix for server TLS certificate (default: "svc")
- `--plm-storage-*` - PLM S3-compatible storage configuration for WAF policies
- `--usage-report-*` - NGINX Plus usage reporting configuration

**Purpose:** Generate self-signed certificates for securing control plane ↔ data plane communication

**Key Functions (certs.go):**

- `generateCA()` - Creates root CA certificate
- `generateCert()` - Creates signed certificates using CA
- `createSecrets()` - Creates/secrets: `server-tls` and `agent-tls` Kubernetes secrets

**Key Flags:**
- `--server-tls-secret` - Name of server TLS secret (default: `server-tls`)
- `--agent-tls-secret` - Name of agent TLS secret (default: `agent-tls`)
- `--service` - Name of Service fronting the Pod
- `--cluster-domain` - DNS domain of Kubernetes cluster (default: `cluster.local`)

- `--overwrite` - Overwrite existing certificates

   - Purpose: Configure NGINX upstream server behavior for specific Services
   - Key settings: Load balancing methods, keep-alive, zone size
   - Supports: 12 load balancing algorithms (round_robin, least_conn, ip_hash, hash, hash consistent, random, random two, random two least_conn, random two least_time=header, random two least_time=last_byte, least_time header/last_byte with/without inflight)

5. **`SnippetsFilter`** - Filter for HTTPRoute/GRPCRoute
   - Purpose: Insert NGINX configuration snippets into HTTPRoute/GRPCRoute contexts
   - Allowed contexts: main, http, http.server, http.server.location

6. **`SnippetsPolicy`** (Direct Attached Policy)
   - Purpose: Insert NGINX configuration snippets into Gateway contexts
   - Similar to SnippetsFilter but targets Gateway resources

7. **`AuthenticationFilter`** - Filter for HTTPRoute/GRPCRoute
   - Purpose: Configure request authentication
   - Auth types: Basic, JWT (NGINX Plus), OIDC (NGINX Plus)
   - Includes authorization rules for token claim validation

8. **`RateLimitPolicy`** (Inherited Attached Policy)
   - Purpose: Local rate limiting for requests
   - Key settings: Rate (r/s or r/m), burst, delay, zone size

9. **`WAFPolicy`** (Inherited Attached Policy)
   - Purpose: Configure F5 WAF for NGINX (App Protect)
   - Sources: HTTP (direct URL), NIM (NGINX Instance Manager), N1C (NGINX One Console), PLM (Policy Lifecycle Manager with S3 storage)
   - Features: Bundle polling, validation (checksum), retry logic, security logs to file/syslog/stderr

### Summary

**CLI Architecture:**
- 5 subcommands: controller (main), generate-certs (TLS), initialize (init container), sleep (lifecycle), endpoint-picker (AI inference extension shim)
- Rich flag set for NGINX Plus, telemetry, Gateway API experimental features, WAF, PLM storage, usage reporting
- Comprehensive validation framework for controller names, ports, endpoints, URLs, and resource names

**API Design:**
- v1alpha1: 9 policy/filter types focusing on traffic control (auth, rate limiting, WAF, client/upstream settings, snippets)
- v1alpha2: 2 infrastructure types (NginxProxy for data plane config, ObservabilityPolicy for tracing)
- waf/v1: PLM integration types for WAF policy/log configuration status
- All types follow Gateway API policy attachment patterns (Direct/Inherited Attached Policies)
